facts.text: Hamilton is home to several historic homes like John Abbott II House, where patriot funds were hidden from the British in 1776.
The Grafton House, once home to the first Sheriff of Mercer County, now hosts events and reflects early county governance.
Kuser Farm Mansion, documented by Helen Almy West in 1954, is one of the few surviving 19th‑century farm estates in the township.
Hamilton’s historic sites include the Isaac Pearson House and the Civil War & Native American Museum, preserving local heritage.
The town’s recreational heart includes Anchor Thread Park and Switlik Park, popular spots for families and cyclists.
Hamilton Library serves as the central hub for local history, offering a comprehensive tour of the township’s colorful past.
